The Shape of Water (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $63,859,435Details
International Box Office $131,931,359
Worldwide Box Office $195,790,794
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $3,476,157
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $7,347,407
Total $10,823,564
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In the hidden high-security government laboratory where she works, lonely Elisa is trapped in a life of silence and isolation. Elisa’s life is changed forever when she and co-worker Zelda discover a secret classified experiment.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$166,564 (0.3% of total gross)
Legs:10.78 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$19,500,000 (worldwide box office is 10.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:2 opening theaters/2,341 max. theaters, 6.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $84,158,502

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 1st, 2017 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
December 22nd, 2017 (Expands Wide) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: January 12th, 2018 (Wide), released as La Forma del Agua (Mexico)
January 19th, 2018 (Wide), released as Shape of Water, The (New Zealand)
January 19th, 2018 (Wide), released as The Shape Of Water (Australia)
January 19th, 2018 (Wide), released as Vandens forma (Lithuania)
January 19th, 2018 (Wide), released as ФОРМА ВОДЫ (Russia (CIS))
Video Release: February 27th, 2018 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for sexual content, graphic nudity, violence and language.
(Rating bulletin 2473 (Cert #51030), 4/26/2017)
Running Time: 123 minutes
Award nomination: 2018 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: BAFTA Best Director Winner, Critics’ Choice Winner Best Director, Critics’ Choice Winner Best Picture, Directors Guild of America Outstanding Directorial Achievement in Theatrical Feature Film Winner, Golden Globe Winner Best Director—Motion Picture, Oscars Best Picture Winner, Producers Guild of America Best Theatrical Motion Picture Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Retro Black and White, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Creature Feature, Dream Sequence, Escape, Interspecies Romance, Romance
Social setting: Cold War, Space Program, Supernatural
Subgenre: Romantic Drama
Time period setting: 1960s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Double Dare You, Fox Searchlight Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

In the hidden high-security government laboratory where she works, lonely Elisa is trapped in a life of silence and isolation. Elisa’s life is changed forever when she and co-worker Zelda discover a secret classified experiment.
